experiment: CLI Effort Level Impact on M&A Risk Assessment Quality

deal_selection.CFLT: IBM acquiring Confluent — clean all-cash tech deal, Low risk baseline
deal_selection.HOLX: Blackstone/TPG acquiring Hologic — PE buyout with CVR, Medium vote risk
deal_selection.UNF: CTAS acquiring UniFirst — Cash & Stock, wide spread, regulatory concern
deal_selection.NVRI: Veolia acquiring Enviri — Cash + Spin-off, widest spread, complex structure
deal_selection.EA: Silver Lake acquiring EA — mega gaming deal, $210/share

quality.note: Quality scores are statistically identical across all effort levels

consistency.note: Zero grade/score differences across all repeat calls at both medium and max effort

diff_details: HOLX: legal Low vs Medium (boundary case — no specific legal threats identified)
NVRI: regulatory Low vs Medium (boundary case — regulatory approval still pending)
grade_agreement.note: All grade differences are on the Low/Medium boundary, a normal variation zone

probability_calibration.note: Probability estimates are within 1-2pp across effort levels. All correctly differentiate: NVRI at 78% (wide spread, complex), CFLT at 93-94% (clean deal)

rationale: Quality is statistically identical across all effort levels (4.86-4.89/5)
Grade agreement is 96%+ across efforts (only boundary Low/Medium differences)
Probability estimates differ by at most 2pp — well within noise
Medium is 84% faster than max (74.7s vs 137.3s per deal)
40-deal overnight run: 50 min at medium vs 92 min at max
100% JSON compliance and 100% consistency at all levels
No evidence that extended thinking improves structured M&A risk assessment
